title: forestiera cartaginensis
intro: Forestiera cartaginensis, also known as the Costa Rican elder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the tropical regions of Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Forestiera cartaginensis include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
